title: Asterisk PJSIP Trunk Registration Failure Trending
app: Custom TA, HEC, or Universal Forwarder mapping Asterisk logs to `asterisk:pjsip`, `asterisk:cdr`, `asterisk:queue_log`, `asterisk:full`, `asterisk:messages`, `asterisk:security`, `asterisk:ami`
spl:
index=voip sourcetype="asterisk:pjsip" earliest=-24h
| eval endpoint=coalesce(endpoint, Endpoint, resource, Resource, contact, Contact, "unknown")
| eval state=coalesce(state, State, status, Status, event, Event, "")
| where match(lower(state), "(?i)unavailable|reject|failed|unregistered|auth.*fail")
| timechart span=1h dc(endpoint) as affected_endpoints count as failure_events
description: Carrier and ITSP trunks require stable PJSIP registrations. Registration loss severs inbound DIDs and can break emergency calling without an obvious application fault.
value: Shortens outages affecting revenue lines, clarifies firewall versus carrier blame, and validates certificate or NAT changes.
implementation: (1) Normalize PJSIP lines to structured fields. (2) Ingest to `voip`. (3) Baseline hourly failures. (4) Alert on sustained elevation.
visualization: Area chart failure_events; table of endpoints; overlay changes.
grandmaExplanation: We watch phone trunk registrations so carrier lines stay connected.
